metadata.title: Motion to Relocate and Modify Parenting Schedule

procedural_facts: I am asking the judge to grant me permission to relocate my minor child, Violette M. Donatello, with me out of state effective June 15, 2026
I am asking the judge to modify the parenting schedule as needed for the move
the Order of Protection I expect to be consolidated into this family case
I therefore give notice of the intended relocation through this filed Motion and formal service
I ask the Court to deem this sufficient notice
I ask the Court to excuse strict compliance with the 60 day notice requirement
I am asking the Court to approve relocation
I am asking the Court to protect my new address from disclosure to Petitioner
I am asking the Court to modify the parenting schedule in Violettes best interests
I am asking the Court to coordinate any relocation orders with existing and requested parenting time restrictions needed for her safety and mental health
I seek permission to relocate Violette with me out of state effective June 15, 2026
I therefore provide notice through this filed Motion and formal service
I ask the Court to deem this sufficient
I ask the Court to otherwise excuse strict compliance with the 60day notice requirement
I also ask the Court to protect the confidentiality of my new residential address under 750 ILCS 5/609.2(c)
I am willing to provide the address to the Court, the Guardian ad Litem, and any professionals or supervisors the Court designates
I request that the address not be disclosed directly to Petitioner
Grant me permission to relocate the minor child, Violette M. Donatello, out of state effective June 15, 2026, pursuant to 750 ILCS 5/609.2
Deem notice by filing and formal service sufficient under the circumstances
excuse strict compliance with the 60day written notice requirement because of the emergency Order of Protection and the short timeline before the move
Protect the confidentiality of my new residential address
permit disclosure only as the Court may order (to the Court, the Guardian ad Litem, and any court approved supervisors)
permit disclosure rather than directly to Petitioner
Modify the parenting schedule and any related allocation provisions as necessary to accommodate relocation
Modify the parenting schedule and any related allocation provisions to protect Violettes best interests
Modify the parenting schedule and any related allocation provisions consistent with any parenting time restrictions the Court enters under 750 ILCS 5/603.10
Enter any additional orders needed to coordinate relocation with existing and requested parenting time restrictions
Enter any additional orders needed to protect Violettes safety, mental health, and stability
Grant any other and further relief the Court deems just and proper

claims.text: The relocation is more than 25 miles from our current home and out of Illinois
claims.body: The relocation is more than 25 miles from our current home and out of Illinois

claims.text: it is a relocation under 750 ILCS 5/600(g)
claims.body: it is a relocation under 750 ILCS 5/600(g)

claims.text: it is a relocation under 750 ILCS 5/609.2
claims.body: it is a relocation under 750 ILCS 5/609.2

claims.text: There is currently an emergency Order of Protection in place protecting me and Violette from Petitioner
claims.body: There is currently an emergency Order of Protection in place protecting me and Violette from Petitioner

claims.text: The emergency Order of Protection has a return date of May 1, 2026
claims.body: The emergency Order of Protection has a return date of May 1, 2026

claims.text: the emergency Order of Protection restricts direct contact and communication
claims.body: the emergency Order of Protection restricts direct contact and communication

claims.text: I cannot safely provide ordinary written notice to Petitioner outside of court
claims.body: I cannot safely provide ordinary written notice to Petitioner outside of court

claims.text: 60 days notice is impracticable in our situation
claims.body: 60 days notice is impracticable in our situation

claims.body: The parties are the parents of the minor child, Violette M. Donatello

claims.text: The parties are subject to a Judgment for Allocation of Parental Responsibilities entered August 3, 2022
claims.body: The parties are subject to a Judgment for Allocation of Parental Responsibilities entered August 3, 2022

claims.body: I am Violettes designated residential parent

claims.body: The relocation is more than 25 miles from my current residence and outside Illinois

claims.text: it makes it a relocation under 750 ILCS 5/600(g)
claims.body: it makes it a relocation under 750 ILCS 5/600(g)

claims.body: it makes it a relocation under 750 ILCS 5/609.2

claims.text: our family lost housing in Kane county
claims.body: our family lost housing in Kane county

claims.text: my husband and I were living in an RV outside of town beginning around July 2024
claims.body: my husband and I were living in an RV outside of town beginning around July 2024

claims.text: the Allocation Judgment defaulted the childrens school to Petitioners address
claims.body: the Allocation Judgment defaulted the childrens school to Petitioners address

claims.text: I was trying to follow the rules and in good faith in our co- parenting structure
claims.body: I was trying to follow the rules and in good faith in our co- parenting structure

claims.text: I believed that living in his apartment would be in their best interest
claims.body: I believed that living in his apartment would be in their best interest

claims.text: I allowed both children to stay with Petitioner while we tried to secure housing in the area
claims.body: I allowed both children to stay with Petitioner while we tried to secure housing in the area

claims.text: I spent part of each week at the RV caring for my husband
claims.body: I spent part of each week at the RV caring for my husband

claims.text: my husband has cirrhosis
claims.body: my husband has cirrhosis

claims.text: my husband is medically fragile
claims.body: my husband is medically fragile

claims.text: I spent part of each week on Petitioners couch so that I could be near the children
claims.body: I spent part of each week on Petitioners couch so that I could be near the children

claims.text: the weather became colder
claims.body: the weather became colder

claims.text: my husbands medical condition worsened
claims.body: my husbands medical condition worsened

claims.text: I had to stay with him more frequently to prevent him from freezing or having a medical crisis
claims.body: I had to stay with him more frequently to prevent him from freezing or having a medical crisis

claims.text: After Thanksgiving 2024, at the beginning of December Petitioner abruptly kicked the children out of his home
claims.body: After Thanksgiving 2024, at the beginning of December Petitioner abruptly kicked the children out of his home

claims.text: Petitioner kicked the children out of his home as a show of control
claims.body: Petitioner kicked the children out of his home as a show of control

claims.text: Petitioner told them to enjoy freezing with your mom
claims.body: Petitioner told them to enjoy freezing with your mom

claims.text: Petitioner used the pretext that I would not denounce the pope
claims.body: Petitioner used the pretext that I would not denounce the pope

claims.text: an allegiance I do not even hold
claims.body: an allegiance I do not even hold

claims.text: This left the children suddenly homeless in winter
claims.body: This left the children suddenly homeless in winter

claims.text: This caused significant emotional harm
claims.body: This caused significant emotional harm

claims.text: with intended risk of physical harm ("enjoy freezing")
claims.body: with intended risk of physical harm ("enjoy freezing")

claims.body: I obtained an Order of Protection in December 2024

claims.body: I could safely remove the children from Petitioners home

claims.text: I could retrieve their belongings

claims.text: I could stabilize them without further confrontation
claims.body: I could stabilize them without further confrontation

claims.text: That was over christmas Break of 2024
claims.body: That was over christmas Break of 2024

claims.body: the children stayed with my mother while we moved everything out

claims.body: the children stayed with my mother while we secured suitable housing

claims.text: our housing situation in this school district remains unstable
claims.body: our housing situation in this school district remains unstable

claims.body: The current landlord allowed us to occupy an uninhabited unit temporarily

claims.body: I have been unable to secure comparable safe, affordable housing in this district

claims.body: The proposed relocation will allow us to move to an area where we have concrete support

claims.text: Violettes recent psychiatric crisis is central to this request
claims.body: Violettes recent psychiatric crisis is central to this request

claims.text: In late March 2026, Petitioner sent Violette a series of extremely disturbing text messages
claims.body: In late March 2026, Petitioner sent Violette a series of extremely disturbing text messages

claims.text: telling her she "should have been aborted"
claims.body: telling her she "should have been aborted"

claims.text: that she is a "piece of shit and a turd that should have been flushed"
claims.body: that she is a "piece of shit and a turd that should have been flushed"

claims.text: that she would be ejected out to the world in shame
claims.body: that she would be ejected out to the world in shame

claims.text: telling her to "GET THE FUCK OUT OF MY TOWN"
claims.body: telling her to "GET THE FUCK OUT OF MY TOWN"

claims.text: This was not the first series of messages like this that she recieved
claims.body: This was not the first series of messages like this that she recieved

claims.text: The school counselor and school police liaison contacted me about these messages
claims.body: The school counselor and school police liaison contacted me about these messages

claims.text: The school counselor and school police liaison encouraged me to report them
claims.body: The school counselor and school police liaison encouraged me to report them

claims.text: The school counselor and school police liaison encouraged me to seek protection
claims.body: The school counselor and school police liaison encouraged me to seek protection

claims.text: I filed Police Report No. 26- 02221
claims.body: I filed Police Report No. 26- 02221

claims.text: I learned that Violette was having suicidal thoughts
claims.body: I learned that Violette was having suicidal thoughts

claims.text: I obtained an emergency Order of Protection on April 10, 2026
claims.body: I obtained an emergency Order of Protection on April 10, 2026

claims.text: that same day arranged for her to be admitted to inpatient psychiatric treatment for suicidal ideation
claims.body: that same day arranged for her to be admitted to inpatient psychiatric treatment for suicidal ideation

claims.text: She has since been discharged from inpatient care
claims.body: She has since been discharged from inpatient care

claims.text: She remains in intensive outpatient treatment

claims.text: She remains in ongoing mental health care
claims.body: She remains in ongoing mental health care

claims.text: structured with the option of virtual participation to accommodate our transition
claims.body: structured with the option of virtual participation to accommodate our transition

claims.text: Relocation with me will place Violette in a safer, more stable environment with her immediate family and extended-family support while she recovers from this crisis
claims.body: Relocation with me will place Violette in a safer, more stable environment with her immediate family and extended-family support while she recovers from this crisis

claims.text: It will also reduce her exposure to Petitioners unpredictable and abusive behavior
claims.body: It will also reduce her exposure to Petitioners unpredictable and abusive behavior

claims.text: It will also allow her to receive consistent care in a setting that is not overshadowed by his presence and threats
claims.body: It will also allow her to receive consistent care in a setting that is not overshadowed by his presence and threats

claims.text: This case arises under the Illinois Parentage Act of 2015
claims.body: This case arises under the Illinois Parentage Act of 2015

claims.text: issues of parenting time and relocation are governed by Part VI of the Illinois Marriage and Dissolution of Marriage Act
claims.body: issues of parenting time and relocation are governed by Part VI of the Illinois Marriage and Dissolution of Marriage Act

claims.text: issues of parenting time and relocation are governed by 750 ILCS 5/602.7 (best interests of the child)
claims.body: issues of parenting time and relocation are governed by 750 ILCS 5/602.7 (best interests of the child)

claims.text: issues of parenting time and relocation are governed by 750 ILCS 5/609.2 (relocation)
claims.body: issues of parenting time and relocation are governed by 750 ILCS 5/609.2 (relocation)

claims.text: Under 750 ILCS 5/609.2, the Court considers,among other things,the circumstances and reasons for the intended relocation
claims.body: Under 750 ILCS 5/609.2, the Court considers,among other things,the circumstances and reasons for the intended relocation

claims.text: Under 750 ILCS 5/609.2, the Court considers the reasons a parent is objecting
claims.body: Under 750 ILCS 5/609.2, the Court considers the reasons a parent is objecting

claims.text: Under 750 ILCS 5/609.2, the Court considers the history and quality of each parents relationship with the child
claims.body: Under 750 ILCS 5/609.2, the Court considers the history and quality of each parents relationship with the child

claims.text: Under 750 ILCS 5/609.2, the Court considers educational opportunities and extended family at the current and proposed locations
claims.body: Under 750 ILCS 5/609.2, the Court considers educational opportunities and extended family at the current and proposed locations

claims.text: Under 750 ILCS 5/609.2, the Court considers the anticipated impact of relocation on the child
claims.body: Under 750 ILCS 5/609.2, the Court considers the anticipated impact of relocation on the child

claims.text: Under 750 ILCS 5/609.2, the Court considers the Courts ability to fashion a reasonable allocation of parental responsibilities and parenting time while minimizing impairment to the parent child relationship caused by relocation
claims.body: Under 750 ILCS 5/609.2, the Court considers the Courts ability to fashion a reasonable allocation of parental responsibilities and parenting time while minimizing impairment to the parent child relationship caused by relocation

claims.text: Violettes recent suicidal ideation shows that safety,stability,and mental health must be central to the relocation decision

claims.body: the emergency Order of Protection based on Petitioners communications show that safety,stability,and mental health must be central to the relocation decision

claims.body: Allowing Violette to relocate with me to a more stable home with immediate and extended family support is in her best interests

claims.text: to live at some distance from Petitioners volatility,is in her best interests
